title: Usuário excluído não autentica (senha e Google)

password_login.test: Não deve autenticar um usuário soft-deleted (login por senha)

google_login.assertion: userOfGoogleId and userOfEmail both filter isDeleted → deleted user invisible; original identity cannot authenticate
google_login.mechanism: InMemoryUserRepository.userOfGoogleId and userOfEmail both apply !user.isDeleted filter (lines 54-56, 50-51 in in-memory-user-repository.ts)
google_login.note: Google path does not return InvalidCredentialsError by design: deleted user is invisible to all lookups, so the system treats the googleId/email as unknown and would create a new account. The original deleted user's session/identity is permanently inaccessible. RF-003 satisfied.

notes: Password login: explicit unit test exists and passes.

Google login: no explicit 'must return InvalidCredentialsError' scenario exists because the usecase does not have a soft-delete guard — instead, the repository filter makes deleted users invisible (treated as non-existent). Two acceptance tests confirm this behavior.
PrismaUserRepository must also apply the same isDeleted filter for production parity — not verified in this task.
